Faults.message:
We're sorry about the power cut in the area. The power cut is affecting some properties in the area shown on the map, but not all. This is why you may notice some of your neighbours have power, but you don't. It is normal to see 1 in 3 homes affected. We are working with our engineers to fix the problem as quickly as possible, and well keep you updated here on PowerTrack.

Engineers have arrived in the area. They are working hard to fix the problem and will get your power back on as quickly as possible. At this stage, we're aiming to have your power back on by 14:00 19/05/2026. If this changes, we'll let you know.

We have had reports of damage to our network. When this happens, the power will automatically turn off to keep everyone safe. Our engineers know exactly where the problem is and are working hard to make the situation safe, repair the wire and get your power back on. These type of faults often require extensive work and specialist teams, such as tree cutters, which is why the restoration time seems so far away. If we can restore power sooner, we promise we will do so.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'TE8856'

The power cut has been caused by a fault on our underground network. Issues like this do happen, and our engineers are very experienced at fixing them. Often, power can be restored quickly with a simple reset or by replacing a fuse at a nearby substation. If the problem is more complicated, repairs may take a little longer but our teams will keep working until everything is safely back up and running. Well keep you updated along the way.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'TE8869'
Faults.name: PO30 Area

Faults.message: Were carrying out essential work on our electricity network in your area today, and our engineers have temporarily turned the power off so this work can be done safely. This work helps make sure the electricity supply to your home or business remains safe and reliable. Depending on the area, this work could include repairs or upgrades to the network, tree cutting near overhead power lines, or connecting new customers to the electricity network.Power will be restored as quickly as possible, and well keep you updated here on Power Track. Your letter provides more details about the specific reason for todays planned power cut, but if you need to speak to us, please call us on 105 or send us a message on Facebook or send us a message on Whatsapp or with reference '250603'.

This is why you may notice some of your neighbours have power, but you don't. It is normal to see 1 in 3 homes affected. We are working with our engineers to fix the problem as quickly as possible, and well keep you updated here on PowerTrack.

We're finding the closest engineer available. They'll be making their way to the area as soon as possible. When they're on their way, we'll let you know what time they'll be arriving. At this stage, we're aiming to have your power back on by17:30 19/05/2026.

This fault is part of a planned power cut which is taking us longer than expected to restore due to a secondary fault we've located. Our teams are still on site and working as hard as they can to get the power back on.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'TE9007'

This is why you may notice some of your neighbours have power, but you don't. It is normal to see 1 in 3 homes affected. We are working with our engineers to fix the problem as quickly as possible, and well keep you updated here on PowerTrack.

Engineers are on the way and will arrive in the area at 08:00 19/05/2026. When they get there, they'll work hard to fix the problem as quickly as possible. We'll keep you updated with any new information. At this stage, we're aiming to have your power back on by 13:30 19/05/2026.

The power cut has been caused by a fault on our underground network. Issues like this do happen, and our engineers are very experienced at fixing them. Often, power can be restored quickly with a simple reset or by replacing a fuse at a nearby substation. If the problem is more complicated, repairs may take a little longer but our teams will keep working until everything is safely back up and running. Well keep you updated along the way.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'TE8858'
Faults.name: IV30 Area

Faults.message: Were carrying out essential work on our electricity network in your area today, and our engineers have temporarily turned the power off so this work can be done safely. This work helps make sure the electricity supply to your home or business remains safe and reliable. Depending on the area, this work could include repairs or upgrades to the network, tree cutting near overhead power lines, or connecting new customers to the electricity network.Power will be restored as quickly as possible, and well keep you updated here on Power Track. Your letter provides more details about the specific reason for todays planned power cut, but if you need to speak to us, please call us on 105 or send us a message on Facebook or send us a message on Whatsapp or with reference '250714'.
